IAS.title: Indian Administrative Service (IAS)
IAS.description: Become an IAS officer through the UPSC Civil Services Examination.

stages.label: Secondary School
tasks: Build strong fundamentals in all subjects
Develop reading habit – newspapers, current affairs
Start basic awareness of Indian polity and history

stages.label: Senior Secondary (Humanities / Any Stream)
tasks: Choose a stream (Humanities preferred but optional)
Read NCERT books for History, Geography, Polity, Economics
Follow daily news and make notes on current affairs
Practice essay and answer writing

stages.label: Bachelor's Degree (Any Discipline)
tasks: Complete graduation in any discipline
Start UPSC foundation course or self-study
Read standard reference books (Laxmikanth, Spectrum, etc.)
Join a test series for Prelims practice
Choose an optional subject and begin preparation

stages.label: UPSC Preparation Phase
tasks: Dedicate full-time to UPSC preparation
Complete Prelims syllabus – GS Paper I & CSAT
Write daily answer practice for Mains (GS I–IV, Essay, Optional)
Attempt mock tests every week
Stay updated with monthly current affairs compilations

stages.label: UPSC Examination & Interview
tasks: Clear UPSC Prelims
Clear UPSC Mains
Prepare for Personality Test (Interview)
Complete LBSNAA training upon selection

Software Engineer.title: Software Engineer
Software Engineer.description: Build a career in software development and engineering.

stages.label: Secondary School
tasks: Strengthen Mathematics and Science fundamentals
Start learning basic programming (Scratch, Python)
Explore computer science concepts through online courses

stages.label: Senior Secondary (PCM / CS)
tasks: Take Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics (and CS if available)
Learn a programming language in depth (Python or Java)
Solve problems on platforms like HackerRank or CodeChef
Prepare for JEE / entrance exams for engineering colleges

stages.label: B.Tech / B.E. in Computer Science or related field
tasks: Master Data Structures & Algorithms
Build 3-5 portfolio projects (web, mobile, or ML)
Complete at least 2 internships at tech companies
Earn certifications (AWS, Azure, or domain-specific)
Contribute to open-source projects on GitHub
Practice competitive programming regularly

stages.label: Entry-Level Software Engineer
tasks: Prepare for coding interviews (LeetCode, System Design)
Apply to product-based companies and startups
Strengthen system design and architecture knowledge
Build a strong LinkedIn and professional network

stages.label: Career Growth & Specialisation
tasks: Deepen expertise in a specialisation (backend, frontend, ML, DevOps)
Lead projects and mentor junior engineers
Pursue M.Tech or MS if targeting research or senior roles
Attend conferences and publish technical articles
Target Senior / Staff Engineer or Engineering Manager track

Data Scientist.title: Data Scientist
Data Scientist.description: Build a career in data science, analytics, and machine learning.

stages.label: Secondary School
tasks: Build strong foundation in Mathematics and Statistics
Learn basics of programming (Python recommended)
Explore data visualisation through simple projects

stages.label: Senior Secondary (PCM)
tasks: Take Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics
Learn Python programming and basic libraries (NumPy, Pandas)
Study probability and statistics in depth
Participate in data science competitions on Kaggle

stages.label: B.Tech / B.Sc. in CS, Statistics, or Mathematics
tasks: Master statistics, linear algebra, and calculus
Learn machine learning algorithms (scikit-learn, TensorFlow)
Complete hands-on projects – EDA, prediction, NLP
Earn certifications (Google Data Analytics, IBM Data Science)
Do internships in data analytics or ML roles
Build a portfolio on GitHub and Kaggle

stages.label: M.Tech / MS or Direct Industry Entry
tasks: Specialise in ML, Deep Learning, NLP, or Computer Vision
Publish research papers or Kaggle competition wins
Master big data tools (Spark, Hadoop, SQL)
Prepare for data science interviews (statistics, ML, coding)

stages.label: Career Growth in Data Science
tasks: Lead data science projects end-to-end
Learn MLOps and model deployment (Docker, Kubernetes, MLflow)
Explore senior roles: Lead DS, ML Engineer, Research Scientist
Mentor teams and contribute to open-source ML projects
Stay current with latest research (papers, conferences)

Doctor.title: Doctor (MBBS)
Doctor.description: Become a medical doctor through MBBS and specialisation.

stages.label: Secondary School
tasks: Build strong fundamentals in Science (Biology, Chemistry, Physics)
Develop a reading and study discipline
Start basic awareness of human biology and health

stages.label: Senior Secondary (PCB)
tasks: Take Physics, Chemistry, Biology
Prepare for NEET-UG entrance examination
Join coaching or follow a structured NEET study plan
Solve previous year NEET papers regularly

stages.label: MBBS (5.5 years including internship)
tasks: Complete MBBS from a recognised medical college
Excel in clinical rotations and hospital internships
Prepare for NEET-PG if pursuing specialisation
Develop bedside manner and patient communication skills

stages.label: MD / MS Specialisation
tasks: Clear NEET-PG and secure a specialisation seat
Complete residency in chosen specialty
Publish research and attend medical conferences
Build clinical experience and professional reputation

stages.label: Practising Doctor / Super Specialisation
tasks: Establish private practice or join a hospital
Pursue DM / MCh for super specialisation if desired
Stay updated with medical advancements (CME credits)
Mentor medical students and contribute to medical education

Product Manager.title: Product Manager
Product Manager.description: Build a career in product management in the tech industry.

stages.label: Secondary School
tasks: Develop strong analytical and communication skills
Learn basic technology concepts and internet literacy
Read about products and businesses you use daily

stages.label: Senior Secondary (Any Stream)
tasks: Choose any stream (Commerce / Science preferred)
Start learning about product thinking and design
Build small projects or participate in hackathons
Develop presentation and public speaking skills

stages.label: Bachelor's Degree (Engineering / Business)
tasks: Study engineering, business, or design
Learn basic programming and data analysis
Do internships in product, business, or tech roles
Take courses on Product Management (Coursera, Reforge)
Build side projects and document product thinking

stages.label: MBA or Direct Industry Entry
tasks: Pursue MBA from a top school (optional but helpful)
Apply for Associate Product Manager (APM) programmes
Practice product case studies and guesstimates
Build a portfolio of product teardowns and analyses

stages.label: Product Manager → Senior PM → Director
tasks: Lead product strategy and roadmap
Master metrics, A/B testing, and data-driven decisions
Build cross-functional leadership skills
Progress to Senior PM, Group PM, or VP Product
Mentor APMs and contribute to product community
